LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS COMPLAINANT PACIFIC HEIGHTS TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON CROSS-COMPLAINT FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF RE DUTY TO DEFEND AND INDEMNITY. CROSS-COMPLAINANT PACIFIC HEIGHTS TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION'S ("PHT") MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF IS GRANTED. ALL REQUESTS FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE ARE GRANTED. PHT MET ITS INITIAL BURDEN BY ESTABLISHING CROSS-DEFENDANT AMY MCLAUGHLIN ("MCLAUGHLIN") ENTERED INTO MULTIPLE AGREEMENTS PROVIDING THAT SHE WOULD INDEMNIFY PHT FOR ANY AND ALL CLAIMS ARISING OUT OF CONSTRUCTION WORK IN HER UNIT AND ADDING PHT AS AN ADDITIONAL INSURED ON REQUIRED INSURANCE POLICIES IN CONNECTION WITH THE REMODEL WORK IN HER UNIT. (UNDISPUTED FACT ("UF") NOS. 5, 7-12.) CONSEQUENTLY, THE BURDEN SHIFTED TO MCLAUGHLIN TO ESTABLISH A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. MCLAUGHLIN HAS FAILED TO DO SO. AS TO THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED SHOWING THAT MCLAUGHLIN WAS NOT AT FAULT FOR OR A SUBSTANTIAL FACTOR IN CAUSING THE DAMAGE TO PLAINTIFF LANA WONG, THIS EVIDENCE IS IRRELEVANT GIVEN THAT THE INDEMNIFICATION PROVISIONS ARE BROADLY WRITTEN AND DO NOT REQUIRE A SHOWING OF FAULT. (SEE CONTINENTAL HELLER CORP. V. AMTECH MECHANICAL SERVICES, INC. (1997) 53 CAL.APP.4TH 500, 503; CENTEX GOLDEN CONSTRUCTION CO. V. DALE TILE CO. (2000) 78 CAL.APP.4TH 992, 997.) THE COMPLAINT IN THE UNDERLYING ACTION ALLEGES THAT SHE WAS DAMAGED BY CONSTRUCTION DUST FROM MCLAUGHLIN'S APARTMENT. MCLAUGHLIN HAS ALSO NOT ESTABLISHED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER PHT WAS ACTIVELY NEGLIGENT. MCLAUGHLIN CITES NO LEGAL AUTHORITY HOLDING THAT A HOMEOWNER'S ASSOCIATION HAS A DUTY TO WARN BUYERS ABOUT POTENTIAL DEFECTS IN A UNIT. (SEE KOVICH V. PASEO DEL MAR HOMEOWNERS' ASSN. ((1996) 41 CAL.APP.4TH 863, 866-67.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PACIFIC HEIGHTS TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS). ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITISCAPE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT GROUP, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION RE: CITISCAPE'S CROSS COMPLAINT RE: SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INDEMNITY AGAINST AMY MCLAUGHLIN. CROSS-COMPLAINANT THE CITISCAPE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT GROUP, LLC'S ("CITISCAPE") MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF IS GRANTED. CITISCAPE MET ITS INITIAL BURDEN BY ESTABLISHING CROSS-DEFENDANT AMY MCLAUGHLIN ("MCLAUGHLIN") ENTERED INTO MULTIPLE AGREEMENTS PROVIDING THAT SHE WOULD INDEMNIFY CITISCAPE, AS AN AGENT AND/OR MANAGER OF PACIFIC HEIGHTS TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION, FOR ANY AND ALL CLAIMS ARISING OUT OF CONSTRUCTION WORK IN HER UNIT. (UNDISPUTED FACT ("UF") NOS. 4, 8.) CONSEQUENTLY, THE BURDEN SHIFTED TO MCLAUGHLIN TO ESTABLISH A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. MCLAUGHLIN HAS FAILED TO DO SO. AS TO THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED SHOWING THAT MCLAUGHLIN WAS NOT AT FAULT FOR OR A SUBSTANTIAL FACTOR IN CAUSING THE DAMAGE TO PLAINTIFF LANA WONG, THIS EVIDENCE IS IRRELEVANT GIVEN THAT THE INDEMNIFICATION PROVISIONS ARE BROADLY WRITTEN AND DO NOT REQUIRE A SHOWING OF FAULT. (SEE CONTINENTAL HELLER CORP. V. AMTECH MECHANICAL SERVICES, INC. (1997) 53 CAL.APP.4TH 500, 503; CENTEX GOLDEN CONSTRUCTION CO. V. DALE TILE CO. (2000) 78 CAL.APP.4TH 992, 997.) THE COMPLAINT IN THE UNDERLYING ACTION ALLEGES THAT SHE WAS DAMAGED BY CONSTRUCTION DUST FROM MCLAUGHLIN'S APARTMENT. MCLAUGHLIN HAS ALSO NOT ESTABLISHED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER CITISCAPE WAS ACTIVELY NEGLIGENT. MCLAUGHLIN CITES NO LEGAL AUTHORITY HOLDING THAT A PROPERTY MANAGEMENT COMPANY HAS A DUTY TO WARN BUYERS ABOUT POTENTIAL DEFECTS IN A UNIT. (SEE KOVICH V. PASEO DEL MAR HOMEOWNERS' ASSN. ((1996) 41 CAL.APP.4TH 863, 866-67.) THE USE OF THE WORD "AGENT" IN THE AGREEMENTS IS NOT AMBIGUOUS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PACIFIC HEIGHTS TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE FIFTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE ADEQUATELY PLED. EVEN IF THE COURT TOOK JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE CONTENT OF THE CC&RS, NONE OF THE CITED SECTIONS IMMUNIZES DEFENDANT FROM LIABILITY. AS TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF THE UNRUH ACT, A CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION IS A "BUSINESS ESTABLISHMENT" UNDER THAT ACT. DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENT THAT THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION LACKS THE REQUISITE ULTIMATE FACTS WAS NOT CONSIDERED BECAUSE THAT ARGUMENT WAS RAISED FOR THE FIRST TIME IN THE REPLY PAPERS. WHILE THE TORT OF ANOTHER DOCTRINE IS NOT A DISTINCT CAUSE OF ACTION BUT MERELY ALLOWS THE RECOVERY OF ATTORNEY'S FEES AS DAMAGES, THERE IS NO POINT IN SUSTAINING THE DEMURRER TO THAT ALLEGED CAUSE OF ACTION. THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION ARE SUFFICIENT TO PERMIT PLAINTIFF TO SEEK RECOVERY OF ATTORNEY'S FEES AS DAMAGES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED. =302/HK

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION (Joinder) In Deft Pacific Heights Towers Condominium Association'S Motion
Off Calendar Mar-04-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Denis S. Kenny, Esq. of Scherer Smith & Kenny LLP, Tel. 415.433.1099, for defendant and moving party Citiscape;
Catherine A. Salah, Esq. of Gordon & Rees LLP, Tel. 415.986.5900, for defendant Pacific Heights;
Richard Wallace, Esq. of Briscoe Ivester & Bazel LLP, Tel. 415.402.2718, for plaintiff;
Richard B. Wilbur, Esq. of Williams Pinetti & Cullen LLP, Tel. 408.288.3868 x107, for defendant Amy McLaughlin.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS COMPLAINANT CITISCAPE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT GROUP, LLC's (JOINDER) IN DEFENDANT PACIFIC HEIGHTS TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCAITION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is Granted in part and denied in part. As to the cause of action for violation of civil rights (Civ. Code sec. 51), summary adjudication is granted. Defendant met its burden by proffering Plaintiff's discovery responses, which show that Plaintiff cannot set forth specific facts establishing that a substantial motivating factor for Defendant's conduct was Plaintiff's race, national origin, or gender. (Material Fact ("MF") nos. 1-2; CACI 3060; Union Bank v. Sup. Ct. (1995) 31 Cal.App.4th 573, 590, holding that a moving defendant may rely on factually devoid discovery responses to shift the burden of proof.) Plaintiff did not meet her burden showing there is a triable issue of material fact. Plaintiff contends that it can be inferred from circumstantial evidence of disparate treatment that Defendant had a discriminatory motive. However, Plaintiff offers no evidence beyond speculation. Further, in reply, Defendant introduces evidence showing that there was a legitimate non-discriminatory reason for the disparate treatment. (Supplemental Declaration of Catherine A. Salah, Exs. E and J.) Defendant also introduces evidence showing that Defendant was reimbursed for the funds it incurred in remediating Unit 1304. (Supp. Decl., Exs. E and I.) The fact that Defendant paid for remediating Unit 1304, but not Plaintiff's unit, is one of the primary facts that Plaintiff argues allows for the inference of discriminatory motive; however, Defendant's evidence indicates this is not the case. The Court may properly consider evidence submitted in reply because Plaintiff did not object to such consideration. (See Gafcon, Inc. v. Ponsor & Assocs. ((2002) 98 Cal.App.4th 1388, 1426, holding that although the moving party may generally not rely on additional evidence filed with its reply papers, an opposing party must object to the submission of such evidence or else it waives that right.) As to the cause of action for tort of another, summary adjudication is denied. As held in the June 6, 2012 Order, "[t]he tort of another doctrine is not a distinct cause of action, but merely allows the recovery of attorney's fees as damages." Under CCP 437c(s), "a claim for damages other than punitive damages" may be determined by summary adjudication only if the parties jointly stipulate to the filing of the motion and the court orders that the motion may be filed. There is no such stipulation or order here. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

Cross-Complainant The Citiscape Property Management Group, LLC's ("Citiscape") motion for summary adjudication on the second cause of action for declaratory relief is granted. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Mar-04-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Catherine A. Salah, Esq. of Gordon & Rees LLP, Tel. 415.986.5900, for defendant and moving party Pacific Heights;
Richard Wallace, Esq. of Briscoe Ivester & Bazel LLP, Tel. 415.402.2718, for plaintiff;
Denis S. Kenny, Esq. of Scherer Smith & Kenny LLP, Tel. 415.433.1099, for defendant Citiscape;
Richard B. Wilbur, Esq. of Williams Pinetti & Cullen LLP, Tel. 408.288.3868 x107, for defendant Amy McLaughlin.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted it tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PACIFIC HEIGHTS TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is Granted in part and denied in part. As to the cause of action for violation of civil rights (Civ. Code sec. 51), summary adjudication is granted. Defendant met its burden by proffering Plaintiff's discovery responses, which show that Plaintiff cannot set forth specific facts establishing that a substantial motivating factor for Defendant's conduct was Plaintiff's race, national origin, or gender. (Material Fact ("MF") nos. 1-2; CACI 3060; Union Bank v. Sup. Ct. (1995) 31 Cal.App.4th 573, 590, holding that a moving defendant may rely on factually devoid discovery responses to shift the burden of proof.) Plaintiff did not meet her burden showing there is a triable issue of material fact. Plaintiff contends that it can be inferred from circumstantial evidence of disparate treatment that Defendant had a discriminatory motive. However, Plaintiff offers no evidence beyond speculation. Further, in reply, Defendant introduces evidence showing that there was a legitimate non-discriminatory reason for the disparate treatment. (Supplemental Declaration of Catherine A. Salah, Exs. E and J.) Defendant also introduces evidence showing that Defendant was reimbursed for the funds it incurred in remediating Unit 1304. (Supp. Decl., Exs. E and I.) The fact that Defendant paid for remediating Unit 1304, but not Plaintiff's unit, is one of the primary facts that Plaintiff argues allows for the inference of discriminatory motive; however, Defendant's evidence indicates this is not the case. The Court may properly consider evidence submitted in reply because Plaintiff did not object to such consideration. (See Gafcon, Inc. v. Ponsor & Assocs. ((2002) 98 Cal.App.4th 1388, 1426, holding that although the moving party may generally not rely on additional evidence filed with its reply papers, an opposing party must object to the submission of such evidence or else it waives that right.) As to the cause of action for tort of another, summary adjudication is denied. As held in the June 6, 2012 Order, "[t]he tort of another doctrine is not a distinct cause of action, but merely allows the recovery of attorney's fees as damages." Under CCP 437c(s), "a claim for damages other than punitive damages" may be determined by summary adjudication only if the parties jointly stipulate to the filing of the motion and the court orders that the motion may be filed. There is no such stipulation or order here. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-06-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RICHARD WALLACE OF BUSCOE IVESTER & BAZEL LLP, TEL. NO. 402-2718 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PACIFIC HEIGHTS TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Overruled in its entirety. The fifth and sixth causes of action are adequately pled. Even if the Court took judicial notice of the content of the CC&Rs, none of the cited sections immunizes defendant from liability. As to the seventh cause of action for violation of the Unruh Act, a condominium association is a "business establishment" under that Act. Defendant's argument that the seventh cause of action lacks the requisite ultimate facts was not considered because that argument was raised for the first time in the reply papers. While the tort of another doctrine is not a distinct cause of action but merely allows the recovery of attorney's fees as damages, there is no point in sustaining the demurrer to that alleged cause of action. The allegations in the eighth cause of action are sufficient to permit plaintiff to seek recovery of attorney's fees as damages. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported. =302/HK
